channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Fini les prix bas : MG lance en France les IM5 et IM6, ses 2 voitures électriques premium face à Xpeng et Mercedes
item.description: Après quelques pays européens, les MG IM5 et IM6 arrivent en France. Intégrées à la gamme MG, ces deux voitures électriques (une berline, un SUV) visent le segment premium avec une débauche de technologies – tout en conservant des tarifs intéressants.

item.title: World Cup streams are intentionally freezing your video player — here is why your VPN’s ad blocker is causing the crash and the way that you can fix it before England vs Mexico
item.description: Streaming services use trackers to check whether you’re watching adverts, meaning ad-blockers can cause problems.

item.title: Quote of the day by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman: 'One of the tech industry’s worst mistakes in a long time was that everybody could go full remote forever' — closing the doors to pandemic-era flexibility
item.description: Despite championing remote working, the technology industry was among the first sectors to row back on this new era

item.title: Uber’s European expansion plans may have hit a speed bump
item.description: Back in February, Uber announced ambitious plans to launch in seven new European markets in 2026 — but now five of those launches are reportedly on hold.

item.title: How to watch Philo outside the USA – stream online and from anywhere with a VPN
item.description: Here's how to watch Philo from outside the United States, and learn how to access it from anywhere abroad with a VPN.

item.title: How to watch Mexico vs England: Free Streams, TV Channels & Kick-Off time for FIFA World Cup 2026 as Three Lions face co-hosts at the Azteca
item.description: Here's how to watch Mexico vs England for free online and from anywhere at the FIFA World Cup 2026 last 16, as Harry Kane & Co. face their biggest test yet.

item.title: Trump memecoin investors lost $3.8 billion, analysis finds
item.description: Nearly 1 million people have lost a total of $3.8 billion after buying President Donald Trump’s $TRUMP memecoin, while Trump made $636 million.
